headline: IES releases RFA on educator workforce and labor markets
blurb: The Institute of Education Sciences prioritizes teacher labor markets, licensure, and retention in its newest funding announcement. Letters of intent due September 1; full proposals in November.

headline: Texas Education Policy Fellows, 2026–27 cohort now open
blurb: A year-long, part-time fellowship for doctoral students and early-career researchers translating evidence into legislative and agency-facing work. Stipend provided; applications close July 31.

headline: Rural Schools & Educator Pipelines (edited volume)
blurb: Seeking 6,000–8,000 word chapters on staffing, "grow-your-own" models, and retention in rural districts. Abstracts due September 15; full drafts in early 2027.

headline: Does the Teacher Incentive Allotment move teachers to high-need campuses?
blurb: A new working paper finds designated teachers are modestly more likely to transfer to — and stay at — high-poverty campuses, with effects concentrated among mid-career teachers.

headline: Early-childhood dual-language programs and third-grade reading
blurb: The study links sustained bilingual program participation to stronger third-grade reading for emergent bilingual students, with the largest gains in two-way immersion settings.

headline: Chronic absenteeism after the return to in-person learning
blurb: Absenteeism remains elevated above 2019 baselines; the paper decomposes trends by grade band and campus poverty and flags early-warning indicators districts can act on.

headline: Texas House interim charges name teacher retention a top study priority
blurb: The Speaker's interim charges direct the Public Education Committee to study compensation, mentoring, and working conditions ahead of the next session — signaling where policy attention is heading.

headline: ED updates guidance on Title II professional-development funds
blurb: Revised guidance clarifies allowable uses for mentoring and induction programs, giving districts more room to fund early-career teacher support.

headline: State board adopts revised instructional materials review process
blurb: The revised process changes timelines and public-comment windows for districts selecting new materials — worth watching for the coming adoption cycle.

headline: Making sense of the new school accountability ratings
blurb: Hosted by a partner policy center (not ERC). July 15, 12–1 pm CT, online. A walkthrough of the updated A–F methodology and what it means for campus improvement planning. Free registration.

headline: Rural education research and practice symposium
blurb: July 22, College Station. A College of Education gathering (not an ERC event) on staffing, transportation, and broadband in rural districts. Open to researchers and district staff.

headline: Southwest Educational Research Association annual meeting
blurb: August 5–7, San Antonio. Regional conference on P–20 research methods and policy. Shared here as an FYI for ERC-affiliated researchers; early-bird registration closes July 20.

headline: Fellowship reviewers wanted: Texas Education Review
blurb: The journal is expanding its reviewer pool across P–12 policy, higher education, and quantitative methods. Rolling sign-up; a light commitment of two to three manuscripts per year.

headline: The long-run earnings effects of CTE course-taking
blurb: Linking high-school CTE participation to workforce records, the study estimates positive earnings returns for industry-aligned pathways, especially in health science and IT.
